what's cheaper, zapier or make — Complete Guide [2026]

AI AI Tools Idea · May 22, 2026 · Updated May 22, 2026
— min read
Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. Learn more →
🚀 AI Platform

As a small business owner, you’re likely struggling to find the time and resources to manage your growing list of tasks and workflows, which is why you’re searching for an answer to the question: what’s cheaper, Zapier or Make. You need a reliable and affordable automation tool to streamline your operations, but with so many options available, it’s hard to know where to start. In this article, we’ll compare Zapier and Make, two popular automation tools, to help you decide which one is the best fit for your business.

First, let’s take a look at the pricing plans for both Zapier and Make. As of 2026, Zapier offers the following plans:

PlanPriceFeatures
Free$0100 tasks per month, 5 zaps
Starter$19.99/month1,000 tasks per month, 20 zaps
Professional$49/month5,000 tasks per month, 50 zaps
Team$299/month50,000 tasks per month, 200 zaps
Company$599/month100,000 tasks per month, 500 zaps

Make, on the other hand, offers the following plans:

PlanPriceFeatures
Free$0100 operations per month, 5 scenarios
Core$29/month1,000 operations per month, 20 scenarios
Pro$49/month5,000 operations per month, 50 scenarios
Business$199/month50,000 operations per month, 200 scenarios
Enterprisecustom pricingcustom features

Based on the pricing plans, Zapier appears to be the more affordable option, especially for small businesses with limited budgets. However, it’s essential to consider the features and limitations of each plan before making a decision.

Now, let’s take a closer look at the pros and cons of using Zapier.

Pros:

  • Easy to use: Zapier has a user-friendly interface that makes it easy to create and manage zaps, even for those with limited technical expertise.
  • Wide range of integrations: Zapier integrates with over 1,000 apps, making it a versatile tool for automating various workflows.
  • Reliable customer support: Zapier offers excellent customer support, including a comprehensive knowledge base, email support, and live chat.
  • Scalable: Zapier’s plans are designed to grow with your business, so you can easily upgrade or downgrade as needed.
  • Try Zapier free to see if it’s the right fit for your business.

Cons:

  • Limited customization options: While Zapier’s zaps are easy to create, they can be limited in terms of customization options, which may not be suitable for complex workflows.
  • Dependence on third-party apps: Zapier’s functionality relies on the APIs of third-party apps, which can be a concern if those apps experience downtime or changes.
  • Limited analytics: Zapier’s analytics capabilities are limited, making it challenging to track the performance of your zaps.
  • Can be overwhelming: With so many integrations available, it can be overwhelming to navigate Zapier’s interface and find the right zaps for your business.
  • Check out Zapier’s pricing plans to determine which plan is best for your business.

Pro tip: When creating zaps, start with simple workflows and gradually add more complexity as needed. This will help you avoid overwhelming yourself with too many options and ensure that your zaps are effective.

Pro tip: Use Zapier’s built-in filtering and sorting features to organize your zaps and make it easier to find the ones you need.

Pro tip: Take advantage of Zapier’s free trial to test its features and see if it’s the right fit for your business before committing to a paid plan.

Who should avoid this: If you’re looking for a highly customizable automation tool with advanced analytics capabilities, Zapier may not be the best fit for your business. Additionally, if you’re already invested in a competing automation platform, such as Make, it may not be worth switching to Zapier, especially if you’ve already developed complex workflows.

In conclusion, Zapier is a reliable and affordable automation tool that’s suitable for small businesses with limited budgets. While it has its limitations, Zapier’s ease of use, wide range of integrations, and scalable plans make it an excellent choice for businesses looking to streamline their workflows.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the main difference between Zapier and Make?

The main difference between Zapier and Make is the level of customization and complexity they offer. Zapier is generally easier to use and more suitable for simple workflows, while Make offers more advanced features and customization options. Additionally, Zapier has a wider range of integrations, with over 1,000 apps, while Make has around 200 integrations.

Can I use Zapier for free?

Yes, Zapier offers a free plan that includes 100 tasks per month and 5 zaps. This plan is suitable for small businesses or individuals with limited automation needs. However, if you need more tasks or zaps, you’ll need to upgrade to a paid plan.

How do I get started with Zapier?

Getting started with Zapier is easy. Simply sign up for a free account, choose the apps you want to integrate, and create your first zap. Zapier’s interface is user-friendly, and its knowledge base and customer support team are available to help you with any questions or issues you may have.

What kind of customer support does Zapier offer?

Zapier offers excellent customer support, including a comprehensive knowledge base, email support, and live chat. Its support team is available 24/7 to help you with any questions or issues you may have, and its knowledge base includes tutorials, guides, and FAQs to help you get the most out of Zapier.
